Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Cross-Defendants Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Apr-29-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ilson W. New, Attorney at Law, Tel. 415.567.7595, for plaintiffs;
Courtney M. Crawford, of Cox Wootton Lerner Griffin Hansen & Poulos LLP, Tel. 415.438.4600, for defendants;
Court Reporter: Carrie Hewerdine, CSR No. 4579, Tel. 415.421.3021

RULING: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS-DEFENDANT SCOTT VARENA'S Motion To Compel Responses To Cross-Defendants Request For Production Of Documents is denied. The Court finds that moving party Varena filed this and the other accompanying discovery motions even after substantial, verified responses had been served by defendant/cross-complainant Duba, and that Mr. Varena's counsel Ilson New did not make a reasonable effort to meet and confer to resolve the perceived discovery disputes. In addition, the moving party has failed to comply with CRC 3.1345(c), which requires that for each discovery request, there must be "a statement of the factual and legal reasons for compelling further responses, answers, or production as to each matter in dispute." The moving papers reiterated the discovery requests and responses as required, but then at the end provided an omnibus statement of legal and factual reasons for compelling further responses to the requests- there was no individualized argument for further response as to each request. This failure is inadequate under CRC 3.1145(c), and is by itself grounds for denial of the motion. Sanctions are awarded against Scott Varena and counsel Ilson New in the total amount of $6,331.60 (for all 10 motions), payable to Duba's counsel Cox, Wootton et al within 10 days of service of this order. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Carrie Hewerdine, CSR No. 4579; Dept. 302.
